preamble: The shift from the centering or rising diphthong [uo] to the high back vowel [u] involves monophthongization and raising, similar to developments in many Germanic and Romance varieties.

examples.note: Development where the diphthong simplifies and raises to the high back position.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
